news.t: Newest Bank of Japan Policymaker Pledges to Keep Careful Eye on Yen
Japanese stocks are on fire. Here's what's driving the hot streak.
Japan Is an Investing Giant for Mom and Pop. It Just Needs to Convince Them.
Nikkei 225 Index nears crucial price as Kioxia, Tokyo Electron, Softbank stocks surge
Nikkei 225 Index slips as Kioxia stock suffers a harsh reversal
Bank of Japan Members Signal Push for Regular Rate Increases to Control Inflation
Top Japan Official Reaffirms Readiness to Bolster Yen
Yen Unlikely to Turn Investment Currency Without Hawkish BOJ Shift
Japan utilities welcome nuclear replacement targets, seek stronger policy support
Why Japan's $70 billion-plus intervention and a rate hike didn't prop up the yen more

holdings.infoBox: FLJP is an equity ETF with a total of 481 individual holdings. The top holdings are Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group stock at 3.43%, Kioxia Holdings at 3.40%, Tokyo Electron at 3.24%, Toyota Motor at 2.82%, and SoftBank Group at 2.62%.

overview.description: The Franklin FTSE Japan ETF (FLJP) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the FTSE Japan RIC Capped index. The fund tracks a market-cap-selected and -weighted index of Japanese equities. FLJP was launched on Nov 2, 2017 and is issued by Franklin Templeton.

data.title: Newest Bank of Japan Policymaker Pledges to Keep Careful Eye on Yen
data.text: Ayano Sato pledged to carefully watch the impact of a weak yen on prices, though she didn't give any clear hints on her monetary policy stance.

data.title: Japanese stocks are on fire. Here's what's driving the hot streak.
data.text: Japanese equities have hit all-time highs at a pace not seen since 1989.

data.title: Japan Is an Investing Giant for Mom and Pop. It Just Needs to Convince Them.
data.text: A shift in Japan's large pool of household financial assets could reshape capital markets, one analyst says.

data.title: Nikkei 225 Index slips as Kioxia stock suffers a harsh reversal
data.text: The Nikkei 225 Index has slumped over the past two consecutive days, reaching a low of 68,863 as fears of a technology bubble intensify. It has slipped by 5.5% from its high this week, with key techno...

data.title: Bank of Japan Members Signal Push for Regular Rate Increases to Control Inflation
data.text: Opinions from the Japanese central bank's recent meeting show a growing sense of worry about inflation and a need to lift interest rates at a steady pace to avoid falling behind the curve.

data.title: Top Japan Official Reaffirms Readiness to Bolster Yen
data.text: Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara reaffirmed that officials will step in as needed to support the yen.

data.title: Yen Unlikely to Turn Investment Currency Without Hawkish BOJ Shift
data.text: An intervention risk for the yen remains elevated but the move alone is unlikely to reverse the currency's weakness, OCBC said.

data.title: Yen Strengthens Amid FX Intervention Risks
data.text: The yen strengthened against other G-10 and Asian currencies amid risks of potential FX intervention by Japanese authorities.

data.title: Japan's Consumer Inflation Held Steady in May
data.text: Consumer inflation remained steady last month, with economists noting that the full effect of higher oil prices has yet to emerge.

data.text: Japan's crude oil import price hit a record high in yen terms in ​May, surpassing April levels, Ministry of Finance data ‌showed on Wednesday, driven by a surge in crude prices resulting from supply d...

data.text: Asian currencies consolidated against the dollar before first decision by a FOMC led by Chairman Kevin Warsh later.

data.text: The Bank of Japan raised interest rates to a 31-year ​high on Tuesday, marking another ‌landmark step in normalising monetary policy as it focused on taming price ​pressures from the energy ​shock cau...

data.title: Bank of Japan Lifts Rates to 31-Year High
data.text: Japan's central bank lifted its policy rate to 1%, anchoring its policy path against the inflationary risks of a war-induced spike in energy costs.

data.title: Japan to send delegation to Greenland to evaluate rare earth extraction, Nikkei says
data.text: Japan is preparing to send a delegation to ​Greenland this summer to evaluate ‌possible rare earth extraction, the Nikkei newswire reported on Sunday.

data.title: Bank of Japan Poised to Raise Rates to 31-Year High
data.text: Japan's central bank is widely expected to raise interest rates to 1% to counter the price shock stemming from the Middle East conflict.

data.title: Bank of Japan set to hike rates to 31-year high, drop hawkish signals
